result: ```python import urllib.request from html.parser import HTMLParser class LinkParser(HTMLParser): def __init__(self): super().__init__() self.links = [] self._current_href = None self._current_text = [] def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs): if tag == "a": attrs_dict = dict(attrs) self._current_href = attrs_dict.get("href") self._current_text = [] def handle_data(self, data): if self._current_href is not None: self._current_text.append(data) def handle_endtag(self, tag): if tag == "a" and self._current_href is not None: self.links.append((self._current_href, "".join(self._current_text).strip())) self._current_href = None self._current_text = [] with urllib.request.urlopen("https://example.com") as response: html = response.read().decode("utf-8") parser = LinkParser() parser.feed(html) for href, text in parser.links: print(f"{text!r:30} -> {href}") ``` **How to run** (no dependencies beyond the stdlib): ```bash python3 script.py ``` Or inline as a one-liner pipe: ```bash python3 -c " import urllib.request from html.parser import HTMLParser class LP(HTMLParser): def __init__(self): super().__init__(); self.links=[]; self._h=None; self._t=[] def handle_starttag(self,tag,attrs): if tag=='a': self._h=dict(attrs).get('href'); self._t=[] def handle_data(self,d): if self._h: self._t.append(d) def handle_endtag(self,tag): if tag=='a' and self._h: self.links.append((self._h,''.join(self._t).strip())); self._h=None with urllib.request.urlopen('https://example.com') as r: html=r.read().decode() p=LP(); p.feed(html) [print(f'{t!r:30} -> {h}') for h,t in p.links] " ``` `example.com` only has one link (`More information...`), so you'll see one line of output. Swap the URL to test against a richer page.
